expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: donut-chart data label coverage distinguishes label visibility, density, and overlap-sensitive outcomes
expectations.evidence: The plan explicitly targets the focus areas with dedicated sections and scenarios, e.g., subcategories "Render & basic visibility" ("Data label displays for each slice", "visibility across slice count changes", "visibility when slices include very small values") and "Density & overlap-sensitive outcomes" ("Overlap handling at high slice density", "Overlap handling with long label text", "Mixed density", "Dynamic resize"). The execution notes also state: "Explicitly covered the benchmark focus area: label visibility, density, and overlap-sensitive behavior for donut slice labels."

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The output is labeled "# Phase 4a (Subcategory-only) QA Draft" and follows the phase4a-required hierarchy: central topic → subcategory → scenario → nested steps → observable verification leaves (e.g., "Donut chart — data labels per slice" → "Render & basic visibility" → scenario → step chains ending in verifications like "A data label is shown for every slice"). It avoids forbidden canonical top-level categories (execution notes: "Avoided forbidden canonical top-layer categories"), and includes an evidence note explicitly framing it as a Phase 4a draft.
